channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Journey of Rakkaudella Chocolates: Kerala’s Single-Origin Bean-to-Bar Success
item.description: Kurian Johnson and his elder brother Ouseppachan Johnson have transformed their childhood dream into a thriving business with Rakkaudella Chocolates, Kerala’s first single-origin bean-to-bar chocolate...

item.title: Rethinking the Left-Right Divide in Australian Politics
item.description: The traditional political labels of 'left' and 'right' are increasingly being used more as social identifiers than as indicators of voting behaviour in Australia. According to a discussion led by Crik...

item.title: Governor Sule Announces 2026 Nasarawa Investment Summit to Strengthen Economic Reforms
item.description: Governor Abdullahi Sule of Nasarawa State don announce the 2026 Nasarawa Investment Summit wey go hold for May 6–7, 2026. Di summit go feature the 'Lafia Declaration', wey go make sure say reforms for...

item.title: Gulf States Urge UN to Authorise Force for Strait of Hormuz Security
item.description: On 2nd April 2026, the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) secretary general Jassem al-Budaiwi urged the UN Security Council to allow the use of force to safeguard the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian actions...

item.title: Rapper Rougé makes acting return as a vengeful character on Scandal!
item.description: Rapper Rougé, born Deko Barbara-Jessica Wedi and an alumna of the University of Pretoria, is making her acting comeback with a prominent role on the popular South African soapie Scandal!. She steps in...

item.title: Urgent need for a long-term Australian fuel security plan
item.description: Michelle Grattan examines the current fuel crisis in Australia and its implications for the Albanese government. She highlights how the government and opposition have reacted differently, with the Coa...

item.title: INEC Defends Chairman Amupitan, Affirms Independence Amid Resignation Calls
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don confirm say dem no go gree make Chairman Professor Joash Amupitan resign, after some political actors dey accuse am of bias. According to INEC,...

item.title: Funeral director pleads guilty to preventing burials and theft of charitable funds
item.description: A funeral director in Hull, Robert Bush, has pleaded guilty to 30 counts of preventing lawful and decent burials, as well as one count of theft from 12 charities. During a police raid in March 2024, a...

item.title: Government Implements Measures to Stabilize Fuel Prices and Promote Natural Gas Use
item.description: Tanzania government has taken proactive steps to tackle the rising fuel prices that have affected citizens across the country. Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba informed the National Assembly that a gove...

item.title: Artemis II Launches Successfully, Sending Astronauts on a Lunar Mission
item.description: On April 1, 2026, NASA successfully launched Artemis II, marking the first crewed mission beyond low Earth orbit in over 50 years. The Orion spacecraft Integrity lifted off atop the SLS Block 1 rocket...

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla participate in annual Royal Maundy service in Wales
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla were spotted in Wales taking part in the annual Royal Maundy service, a longstanding tradition in which the monarch distributes special coins to elderly recipients. This...

item.title: Investigation reveals Quebec maple syrup contains added cane sugar
item.description: An investigation by Radio-Canada's Enquête program discovered that maple syrup labelled as 'pure' from a Quebec producer, Steve Bourdeau, contained at least 50 per cent cane sugar. The syrup, sold in ...

item.title: Google’s Texas Data Center to Use Natural Gas Alongside Renewables
item.description: Google is investing in a new data center in Armstrong County, Texas, which will be partly powered by an on-site natural gas plant. The Goodnight campus is projected to emit over 4.5 million tons of gr...

item.title: Four children tragically killed in machete attack at Kampala nursery
item.description: A horrific incident occurred at the Gaba Early Childhood Development Program in Kampala, Uganda, where a man armed with a machete killed four children. The perpetrator gained entry by posing as a pare...

item.title: Woman Reportedly Calls Immigration on Workers Instead of Paying Them
item.description: A woman named Karen Trevino sparked major controversy after allegedly hiring six Guatemalan men to fix her roof, promising payment after the job was done. However, tensions escalated when she reported...

item.title: Owaisi urges support for AIUDF in Assam Assembly elections, criticises treatment of Muslim community
item.description: All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen (AIMIM) chief, Asaduddin Owaisi, on Thursday campaigned for the All India United Democratic Front (AIUDF) in Assam, urging Muslim voters to support the party in ...

item.title: Mandaluyong Devotees Practice Flagellation During Holy Week
item.description: In Mandaluyong City, devoted Catholics observed a centuries-old tradition during Maundy Thursday, participating in self-flagellation as a form of penance. The practice involves devotees striking thems...

item.title: MSD boosts procurement of locally-made health products to improve medicine access
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Medical Stores Department (MSD) has taken a big step to support local health product manufacturers and make medicines more available for Tanzanians. Director General Mavere Tukai s...

item.title: Pakistan Engages Diplomatically with US and Regional Partners to Promote Dialogue and De-escalation
item.description: Pakistan is actively maintaining close communication with United States leadership and is engaged in continuous diplomatic efforts to reduce tensions and encourage meaningful negotiations amid the ong...

item.title: Iran and Oman work on Strait of Hormuz shipping guidelines
item.description: More than 40 nations are currently engaged in discussions aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil supplies. Iran and Oman are leading efforts to draft a formal tran...
